diagnostics.interpretation: Rupture aux jonctions de détecteurs, calibration locale ou sonde différente.
diagnostics.label: Splice / raccord détecteurs

diagnostics.interpretation: Décalage systématique entre campagnes, instruments ou référence blanche.
diagnostics.label: Erreur calibration / référence blanche

diagnostics.interpretation: Effet systématique du support, blanc/noir, transflectance ou environnement de mesure.
diagnostics.label: Fond différent

diagnostics.interpretation: Artefacts numériques ou traitement spectral incorrect.
diagnostics.label: Erreur interpolation / rééchantillonnage

diagnostics.interpretation: Combinaison possible changement de sonde + splice, amplifiée par géométrie, fond ou calibration.
diagnostics.label: Signature VERA25-like

diagnostics.interpretation: Modification de l'illumination, collecte, angle ou distance sonde-échantillon.
diagnostics.label: Différence de sonde / géométrie

diagnostics.interpretation: Couverture partielle du spot; contribution du fond ou du support.
diagnostics.label: Mélange feuille + fond

diagnostics.interpretation: Variété, espèce, lot ou condition différente mais physiquement plausible.
diagnostics.label: Spectre hors domaine valide

This is a greenish rock containing only 20% quartz, 35% albite, a mixture of chlorite and muscovite (40%) and 5% rutile.Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.fine.gneiss1.spectrum.txt

This is a very coarse-grained rock composed of porphyroblasts of garnet frequently enclosing embayed remnants of tectosilicates and opaques. Larger grains of heavily altered alkali feldspar (oligoclase and K-spar) and quartz make up the bulk of the remainder of this rock.Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.fine.gneiss2.spectrum.txt

This is a coarse-grained sample composed of hornblende euhedra assocciated with biotite, with anhedra and subhedra of alkali feldspars (oligoclase and orthoclase) which are slightly sericitized. Zircon and pyrite crystals occur throughout the section. There may be a trace of quartz present as well. Some carbonate (?), or zircon masses (high relief, colorless, perhaps 3rd order B.R.) occur in this rock, often associated with the amphibole or opaques. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.fine.gneiss7.spectrum.txt

This is a fine-grained to microcrystalline sample showing relict bedding. It consists of a pleochroic greenish brown phase--probably epidote, and cordierite or andalusite. The latter occur as the coarser grains in veins parallel to the bedding, as well as finer material along with the epidote in the groundmass. In addition there are scattered grains of calcite and opaques, along with veinlets of carbonaceous material which again parallel the bedding. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.hornfels.fine.hornfe1.spectrum.txt

This is a greenish, very fine-grained to microcrystalline rock containing somewhat larger recrystallized feldspar and carbonate grains. The finer material or groundmass also consists of these two phases in addition to a greenish microcrystalline mineral (chlorite?)Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.hornfels.fine.hornfe2.spectrum.txt

This medium to coarse-grained rock is composed of well compacted rounded to subrounded strained quartz grains showing pressure solution effects, with some silica recrystallization and fluid inclusions present. In the remaining small interstices and rimming the quartz is what appears to be fine to microcrystalline chlorite. Additionally there are scattered opaques present in this section. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.fine.qrtzit6.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained quartzite composed of subrounded equant quartz with some secondary silica (chalcedony) and quartz deposition, sometimes the chalcedony is pseudomorphic to quartz grains. These quartz grains contain numerous fluid inclusions, are often strained and occasionally show pressure solution effects. Ferric material (plus clay?) is scattered throughout the sample giving this rock its reddish hue. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.fine.qrtzit3.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained somewhat foliated rock. Bands of strained quartz with pressure solution effects alternate with irregular bands of biotite which have usually been altered to chlorite, associated with abundant magnetite and ferruginous staining. Much less muscovite, usually as sericite, is also present. Green to slightly blue-green epidote crystals occur commonly throughout the rock. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.fine.schist3.spectrum.txt

This is a coarse-grained greenish schistose rock containing red garnet porphyroblasts. Microscopically there are granulated grains of actinolite and tremolite with somewhat lesser amounts of chlorite. This latter mineral is apparently secondary to the amphiboles and their granulation. It often occurs as an alteration around the garnet crystals in addition to its association with the amphiboles. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.fine.schist7.spectrum.txt

Sample is moderately sorted fine medium-grained rock with slight porosity. Well-rounded to subrounded quartz grains have fluid inclusions and ferruginous staining, and are cemented with overgrowths of chalcedony. A trace of microcline and regions of concentrated carbonaceous granules and/or opaques on the grains are present. Secondary carbonate crystallites exist in some pores. Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.sandstone.fine.sandst1.spectrum.txt

The sample is a well-sorted, medium fine-grained, somewhat compacted sandstone. The strain-free, angular to subangular quartz grains are in a matrix of sericite, glauconite (mostly as autochthonous microcrystalline masses), a yellowish-brown microcrystalline material (clay and/or glauconite), with some muscovite, biotite, carbonate and minor microcline and opaques. Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.sandstone.fine.sandst2.spectrum.txt

This low porosity sandstone contains fine-grained, well-sorted subangular quartz set in a ferruginous clay matrix. There has been a moderate amount of silica recrystallization, and in places, it acts as silica cement In addition, there has been minor pressure solution of the quartz. There are scattered opaques, brown and green glauconite, zircon, and a trace of microcline. Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.sandstone.fine.sandst6.spectrum.txt

A well-sorted sample, consisting of medium fine-grained subangular to subrounded quartz grains set in an abundant matrix of yellowish-brown clay (kaolinite and/or illite?). The quartz grains contain numerous fluid inclusions, and there are trace amounts of plagioclase, tourmaline (schorlite), microcline, and opaques.Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.shale.fine.shale1.spectrum.txt

Sample consists of a brownish fine-grained to microcrystalline groundmass with recrystallized(?), foraminiferid tests. Tests are biserial, uniserial, planispiral/trochospiral and fusilinid types. The groundmass consists of brown clay, carbonate, and lesser amounts of fine quartz. Additionally, there are scattered flecks and small lenses of opaque material. Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.shale.fine.shale3.spectrum.txt
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
